Development Comments: CRITICAL race condition: when users submitted bug reports, attachments from different submissions were all being uploaded onto a single (older) bug. Root cause: the submitReport controller used raw ZCQL INSERT (which doesn't return the new ROWID) and then did a separate SELECT ... ORDER BY ROWID DESC LIMIT 1 to fetch the new row. Because Catalyst's DataStore is eventually consistent, this follow-up SELECT often returned a stale older row, and the frontend then uploaded attachments against that wrong ROWID. Fix: switched to atomic catalystApp.datastore().table(BUG_REPORTS).insertRow(...) which returns the new ROWID directly from the INSERT response. Also added defensive validation on the frontend to refuse uploads if no ROWID comes back.
Regression Testing Comments: Submit two bug reports in quick succession with different attachments each. Open both - each should only contain its own attachments. Cross-user test: two users submit at the same time, attachments must not cross over.

Development Comments: When opening a bug detail, the attachment image used to never actually load - the spinner just sat there indefinitely. Root cause: the was being kept in display:none (Tailwind 'hidden' class) while the loaded state was false. Browsers do NOT fetch images that are display:none - they defer the request until visibility changes - so the fetch never started, onLoad never fired, and the skeleton remained forever. Fix: always render the in the DOM with opacity-0 (not hidden) while loading; the skeleton overlays via absolute positioning. Also bumped to loading='eager' + fetchpriority='high' since attachments inside an open modal are above-the-fold and should fetch immediately. Same fix applied in both the user detail modal and the super-admin slider.
Regression Testing Comments: Open any bug with an image attachment - the image should load within a normal CDN response time, with a brief skeleton then smooth fade-in. Should not get stuck on the spinner. Broken URLs should show a 'Could not load' card with an alert icon.

Development Comments: In the super-admin Bug Reports list, recent reports were appearing below older ones. Example: a report created 3 minutes ago (REPORT-1622383) was rendered BELOW one created 18 hours ago (REPORT-1623021). Root cause: sort was by ROWID DESC, but Catalyst's ROWIDs are not strictly monotonic with creation order on this tenant (the project pools ROWIDs across many tables, so bug_reports inserts pick up non-sequential IDs). Fix: sort by CREATEDTIME with ROWID as tiebreaker. The createdTime helper handles both formats Catalyst returns (numeric epoch ms or ISO string). The 'New Reports' tab benefits from the same sort. 'Replies' tab is unchanged (still sorts by reply time).
Regression Testing Comments: Submit a fresh bug as a regular user. As super admin, go to Bug Reports > All - the new bug should sit at the top of the list. Verify the rest of the list is in descending creation-time order.
